# launchdarkly_model_config (Resource)

Provides a LaunchDarkly model config resource.

This resource allows you to create and manage AI model configurations within your LaunchDarkly project. Since the API does not support updates, any field change will force recreation of the resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "launchdarkly_model_config" "example" {
  project_key          = launchdarkly_project.example.key
  key                  = "gpt-4-turbo"
  name                 = "GPT-4 Turbo"
  model_id             = "gpt-4-turbo"
  model_provider       = "openai"
  cost_per_input_token = 0.00001
  tags                 = ["production"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) The model config's unique key.
- `model_id` (String) The model identifier (e.g. `gpt-4`, `claude-3`).
- `name` (String) The model config's human-readable name.
- `project_key` (String) The project key.

### Optional

- `cost_per_input_token` (Number) The cost per input token for the model.
- `cost_per_output_token` (Number) The cost per output token for the model.
- `custom_parameters` (String) A JSON string representing custom parameters.
- `icon` (String) The icon for the model config.
- `model_provider` (String) The provider name (e.g. `openai`, `anthropic`).
- `params` (String) A JSON string representing model parameters.
- `tags` (Set of String) Tags associated with the model config.

### Read-Only

- `global` (Boolean) Whether the model config is available globally.
- `id` (String) The ID in the format `project_key/key`.
- `version` (Number) The version of the model config.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# LaunchDarkly model configs can be imported using the format `project_key/model_config_key`
terraform import launchdarkly_model_config.example example-project/gpt-4-turbo
```
